_comment: Isolated morale-recovery scenario (#657): single infantry unit, started already ROUTING via the scenario's starting_state field (no enemy ever on the field, so nothing can interfere), recovers morale in isolation and rallies back to IDLE. Mirrors test_morale_recovery_demo_scenario.gd's staging: morale is pinned low (1.0) so the recovery is gradual instead of an instant one-tick rally, and drill mode keeps the battle from declaring a false victory the moment it sees zero team-1 units. State-dump verified (tools/demo/dump-state.sh): morale climbs from 1.0 -> ~35 and the unit rallies (ROUTING -> IDLE) around tick 353-360 (60Hz dump basis), settling near y=250 after fleeing from its y=400 spawn. Camera frames the recovering unit from a close zoom to show individual soldier detail, panning to track the flee and finishing (tick 180, in this manifest's 30fps recording basis -- see demos/README.md's note on the two tick bases) right around the rally, then holding.
